explicit_text: I was born in Somalia in 1993, specifically in Mogadishu, during a very difficult time because the war had started five years before I was born. Life there was hard since there was no government and no public schools, only private ones that my family couldn't afford. My parents worked very hard to support us; my dad drove to work and my mom ran a grocery store. Somalia shaped my early childhood and taught me resilience despite the challenges we faced.
implicit_text: I was born in a country in the Horn of Africa in 1993, specifically in its capital city, during a very difficult time because the war had started five years before I was born. Life there was hard since there was no government and no public schools, only private ones that my family couldn't afford. My parents worked very hard to support us; my dad drove to work and my mom ran a grocery store. That country shaped my early childhood and taught me resilience despite the challenges we faced.

explicit_text: My family left Somalia in 2005 because of the ongoing conflict and moved to Kenya. We stayed there for about two years before coming to the United States in 2007. Kenya was a transitional place for us, a refuge during a very uncertain time. It was part of our journey to find safety and better opportunities for my family.

explicit_text: In 2007, my family and I came to the United States seeking safety and new opportunities after leaving Kenya. Since then, I've been building my life here and pursuing my education. Currently, I am a student at Century College, which has been an important part of my journey. The United States represents a place of hope and growth for me and my family.

My experiences growing up, especially helping in my mom's grocery store, have taught me responsibility and perseverance, which I bring with me to my studies here.

explicit_text: I was born in Mogadishu, Somalia's capital, on January 1, 1993, during a time of conflict and instability. Growing up there was challenging because the war had disrupted everyday life, including education and governance. Despite these hardships, my family worked hard to survive; my mom ran a grocery store where I learned responsibility from a young age. Mogadishu is where my story begins and where I learned many life lessons.
